category_title: Students with limited English proficiency who communicate knowledge through visual arts
minimal_context: The student is in the regular classroom attempting to write five paragraphs on a topic to communicate their knowledge.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please write five paragraphs on your topic to communicate your knowledge.

STUDENT: Five paragraphs? I don't know enough words for that. Plus, it would be boring with just words on everything.

TUTOR: Just do your best to write it down.

STUDENT: Can I draw an elaborate comic strip instead? Some really don’t explain with words. They teach more with pictures.

TUTOR: Keep trying to write the paragraphs for now.

STUDENT: (Puts pencil down) My vocabulary is lacking. I can't write five paragraphs about this.

options.A: Establishing a consistent two-week routine for every writing unit to complete the five paragraphs.
options.B: Delivering twelve hour-long sessions, scheduled twice-weekly, of a specialized mindfulness intervention program for the student.
options.C: Publicly celebrating awards and improvements (like increased vocabulary usage) as often as possible in the classroom.
options.D: Offering options for summative presentation topics with open-ended format possibilities for how they present new knowledge.
